methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Sagar Defence Engineering, a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nikunj Parashar, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25.4M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $51M. Peer-median analysis draws from four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $400M, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion (range: $150M to $29.0B, with Scale AI as a material outlier).

The triangulated analysis produces a median valuation of $225M, supported by a low bound of $43M and a high bound of $460M. The wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty in the peer set: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively near the low end, while the peer median pulls substantially higher. Key sensitivities include the relative weight assigned to outlier comparables and whether Scale AI's $29B valuation represents a true peer or a structural outlier. Additional uncertainty stems from limited visibility into Sagar's revenue scale, customer concentration, and differentiation relative to the peer cohort. A valuation in the $150M–$300M range may be most defensible absent further disclosure on these operational metrics.
